perturbation.original_text: Sponsor desires to have certain * * * rights with respect to vitamins and nutritional supplements on the drkoop.com Website and to be the * * * vitamin and nutritional supplement tenant in the E-Commerce area of the drkoop.com Website and drkoop.com desires to promote Sponsor for vitamin and nutritional supplements and to make Sponsor its' * * * vitamin and nutritional supplement tenant pursuant to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor desires to have certain * * * rights with respect to vitamins and nutritional supplements on the drkoop.com Website and to be the * * * vitamin and nutritional supplement tenant in the E-Commerce area of the drkoop.com Website and drkoop.com desires to promote Vitamin Shoppe Industries for vitamin and nutritional supplements and to make Sponsor its' * * * vitamin and nutritional supplement tenant pursuant to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Sponsor' with the full company name 'Vitamin Shoppe Industries', we create ambiguity. Throughout the document, Sponsor is presumed to refer solely to Vitamin Shoppe Industries, Inc. However, this perturbation introduces a potential interpretation that the 'Sponsor' could also refer to other entities besides Vitamin Shoppe Industries.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1. * * * VITAMIN SPONSOR. Throughout the Term (as defined below), Sponsor shall be the * * * vitamin and supplement sponsor of, and the * * * vitamin and supplement advertiser on, the drkoop.com Website * * *
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Sponsor' with 'Vitamin Shoppe Industries' in one instance, while the contradicted text, Section 1.1, defines 'Sponsor' as the '* * * vitamin and supplement sponsor of, and the * * * vitamin and supplement advertiser on, the drkoop.com Website * * *'. By using the full company name in one instance, the contract introduces ambiguity as to whether 'Sponsor' refers only to Vitamin Shoppe Industries, Inc. or potentially other entities. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency because it undermines the defined role of 'Sponsor' as described in Section 1.1.

perturbation.original_text: Drkoop.com requests its users ("Individual Users"), to provide personal information when they sign up for certain services including requesting information on a specific disease, chat rooms and forums ("User Data"). Such User Data is owned by each Individual User and drkoop.com does not use or disclose any such User Data without the consent of the Individual User.
perturbation.changed_text: Drkoop.com requests its users ("Customers"), to provide personal information when they sign up for certain services including requesting information on a specific disease, chat rooms and forums ("User Data"). Such User Data is owned by each Customer and drkoop.com does not use or disclose any such User Data without the consent of the Customer.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Individual Users" with "Customers" creates confusion. The contract defines "Individual Users" specifically, but using "Customers" introduces a broader, undefined term. This raises questions about whether all users are customers and how this affects data ownership and consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Drkoop.com shall provide to Sponsor any and all User Data for which the Individual User has specifically authorized release to Sponsor. In the event that an Individual User grants rights to Sponsor for use of his User Data, Sponsor shall use its best efforts to keep User Data confidential and shall only use such data in an ethical manner. Sponsor may use User Data for its owns purposes, but User Data may not be disclosed, sold, assigned, leased or otherwise disposed of to third parties by Sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Individual Users," and the contradicted text refers to this defined term when specifying how Drkoop.com shares User Data with Sponsors. By changing "Individual Users" to "Customers," which is not a defined term, the change creates ambiguity about which users' data the contradicted text refers to, potentially conflicting with the consent process for sharing data, as it's unclear if "Customers" encompasses all "Individual Users" or a subset. This creates a misaligned terminology in text contradiction according to the definitions given.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Sponsor is hereby authorized during the Term to use the logo and tag lines set forth on Exhibit D, on its Site, in its catalogs and in its stores in connection with its marketing and promotion efforts, in each case in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and subject to the reasonable approval of drkoop.com. Sponsor is hereby authorized to place such logo and any one of such tag lines on its Site, in its stores and in its catalogs in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Vitamin Shoppe Industries is hereby authorized during the Term to use the logo and tag lines set forth on Exhibit D, on its Site, in its catalogs and in its stores in connection with its marketing and promotion efforts, in each case in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and subject to the reasonable approval of drkoop.com. Sponsor is hereby authorized to place such logo and any one of such tag lines on its Site, in its stores and in its catalogs in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Sponsor' with the full company name 'Vitamin Shoppe Industries', on the first sentence, we are creating ambiguity. Throughout the document, Sponsor is presumed to refer solely to Vitamin Shoppe Industries, Inc. However, this perturbation introduces a potential interpretation that the 'Sponsor', reffered after, could also refer to other entities besides Vitamin Shoppe Industries. This is causing and In-Text Contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor represents and warrants for the benefit of drkoop.com that the Sponsor Advertising Content and Sponsor Marks are true and correct and do not and will not for the Term infringe upon or violate: (i) any intellectual property rights, including any copyright or trademark rights, of any third party and do not and will not constitute a defamation or invasion of the rights of privacy or publicity of any kind of any third party, (ii) any applicable law, regulation or non-proprietary third-party right. Sponsor further represents and warrants for the benefit of drkoop.com that the Sponsor Advertising Content does not contain any material which is unlawful, harmful, abusive, hateful, obscene, threatening or defamatory and Sponsor is not an entity or an affiliate of any entity which engages in the manufacture or wholesale distribution of tobacco or tobacco products (such activities are collectively referred to herein as "Tobacco Industry Affiliation").
perturbation.justification: The change introduces ambiguity by replacing 'Sponsor' with 'Vitamin Shoppe Industries' in the first instance but retaining 'Sponsor' in subsequent references. The contradicted text places representations and warranties on 'Sponsor.' The perturbation creates uncertainty whether those representations and warranties apply only to Vitamin Shoppe Industries, Inc., or to a potentially broader group of entities, creating ambiguity that weakens the force of the representations. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
